key_insight: Direct spraying of chemically polymerized polypyrrole onto alginate nonwoven fabrics creates a flexible, homogeneous conductive coating with strong photothermal water-evaporation performance.
engineering_relevance: This offers a simple, scalable coating route for solar-driven water treatment textiles that can also handle colored wastewater.
method: Prepared a PPy dispersion by chemical oxidative polymerization
Applied PPy onto alginate nonwoven fabrics via direct spraying
Characterized coating formation and adhesion with FTIR, XPS, SEM, and EDS
evidence_signal: At a Py dosage of 0.3 mol m−2, the PPy/ANF achieved 1.73 kg m−2 h−1 evaporation rate and 93.54% solar-thermal conversion efficiency under 1 sun.
watch_item: Performance depends on coating dosage and wastewater chemistry, so dye type, pigment size, and surfactant choice can noticeably affect evaporation.
paper_title: Spraying Polypyrrole Coatings on Alginate Nonwoven Fabrics and Investigating Their Conductive and Photothermal Properties
